title: may / might (possibility)
examples: Since alcohol is often used socially, less in-person time with friends may mean less drinking.

title: have to / must
examples: Monthly in-person social time dropped from 30 hours in 2003 to just 10 hours in the year 2020, especially for ages 15 to 24, a decline made worse by the COVID-19 pandemic. On top of that, mocktails and non-alcoholic options like beer, wine, and liquor are becoming more popular, giving people other choices when they do get together. If you're celebrating, you have drinks, it's been a part of my life. I have one or two drinks here and there, and I don't feel it affects me, you know, in a negative way, health wise or anything like that. Everybody has to do what's right for them personally. That's my own little thought on it. If you start becoming dependent on it, if it's starting to rule your life for a little bit, then you're drinking way too much. You guys have a great day or a great evening. Bye-bye.
